---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true     # single self-contained file; no external assets
    theme: cosmo
params:
  data_file: "data/sample.csv"
  x_axis_var: null            # null = auto-detect via TIME_KEYWORDS
  report_title: null          # null = derive from filename stem
  cat_unique_max: 10
  suppress_levels_above: 20
  output_dir: "."
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pathlib
import pandas as pd

from eda.loader import load_dataset
from eda.classify import classify_dataset
from eda.plots import categorical_panel, continuous_panel, single_var_plot
from eda.summary import summary_table

# Resolve parameters
data_path   = pathlib.Path(params["data_file"])
report_title = params["report_title"] or data_path.stem

# Load and classify
df, column_labels = load_dataset(data_path)
classified = classify_dataset(
    df,
    column_labels=column_labels,
    x_axis_var=params["x_axis_var"] or None,
    cat_unique_max=params["cat_unique_max"],
    suppress_levels_above=params["suppress_levels_above"],
)
```

# `{python} report_title`

---

## 1. Overview

```{python}
#| label: overview
#| echo: false

# TODO: render a summary card showing:
#   - File name, size, format
#   - Dimensions (rows × columns)
#   - Variable counts by type (continuous, categorical, logical, suppressed)
#   - Parameters used for this render
#   - Data quality flags:
#       * All-missing columns
#       * Near-zero-variance columns (single unique non-null value)

print(f"Dataset: {data_path.name}")
print(f"Dimensions: {df.shape[0]:,} rows × {df.shape[1]:,} columns")
print(f"Continuous:  {classified.n_continuous}")
print(f"Categorical: {classified.n_categorical} "
      f"({classified.n_logical} logical, {classified.n_suppressed} suppressed)")
```

---

## 2. Categorical & Logical Variables

```{python}
#| label: categorical-panel
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: !expr "max(4, 3 * ((classified.n_categorical - classified.n_suppressed + 1) // 3))"

# TODO: render categorical_panel(classified)
# categorical_panel(classified)
```

*Variables with > `{python} params["suppress_levels_above"]` unique levels are
excluded from this panel but included in the Data Summary table.*

---

## 3. Continuous Variables

```{python}
#| label: continuous-panel
#| echo: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: !expr "max(4, 3 * ((classified.n_continuous + 1) // 3))"

# TODO: render continuous_panel(classified)
# continuous_panel(classified)
```

*X-axis: **`{python} classified.x_axis_var`***

---

## 4. Variable Detail

```{python}
#| label: variable-detail
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

# TODO: render one tabbed section per variable using single_var_plot()
# Use Quarto tabset syntax:
#   ::: {.panel-tabset}
#   ## VariableName
#   <figure + inline stats>
#   :::

all_vars = classified.continuous + classified.categorical
for var in all_vars:
    print(f"\n### {classified.column_labels.get(var, var)}\n")
    # single_var_plot(df, var, classified)
    # TODO: print inline statistics (n, n_missing, type-specific stats)
```

---

## 5. Data Summary

```{python}
#| label: data-summary
#| echo: false

# TODO: render summary_table(classified)
# summary_table(classified)

# Placeholder: plain describe() until summary_table() is implemented
df.describe(include="all").T.reset_index().rename(columns={"index": "variable"})
```
